skill.name: obsidian-markdown
skill.description: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown notes with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, YAML properties, tags, comments, math, diagrams, and footnotes.
skill.summary: Write Obsidian-ready Markdown notes with vault links, embeds, callouts, and structured properties.
skill.author: kepano

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backticks, URLs, and keyword matches, but review found documentation examples only. No executable scripts, command invocation, secret access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed Markdown files.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution patterns are Markdown code fences and inline code examples. They document Obsidian syntax and do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected files contain Markdown documentation and examples. I found no script file, shell invocation, runtime hook, or user input path that could execute these backticks.

low_findings.title: Static Network URL False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported network indicators are Markdown links and image examples. They do not perform requests or instruct the assistant to transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as Markdown link targets to example.com and Obsidian documentation. No fetch, curl, webhook, or network-capable code was present.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Reconnaissance And Crypto False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance and weak crypto hits are keyword collisions in Markdown prose and examples, such as file extensions, block identifiers, Mermaid syntax, and note content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is explanatory Obsidian Markdown content. I found no cryptographic API use, host discovery command, network scan, or system inventory behavior.

content.value_statement: Obsidian notes often need syntax that normal Markdown tools miss. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create notes with valid wikilinks, embeds, callouts, and properties.
actual_capabilities: Creates Obsidian notes with YAML properties, tags, aliases, and cssclasses.
Formats internal links with wikilinks, heading links, block links, and display text.
Adds embeds for notes, headings, blocks, images, PDFs, audio, and search results.
Uses Obsidian callout syntax, including custom titles, folding, nesting, and common callout types.
Applies Obsidian-specific formatting such as comments, highlights, math, Mermaid diagrams, and footnotes.
limitations: It does not validate note rendering inside a live Obsidian vault.
It does not manage plugins, themes, sync settings, or vault configuration.
It assumes standard Markdown knowledge and focuses on Obsidian-specific syntax.
It cannot confirm that linked note names or attachment files exist unless the vault files are available.

use_cases.title: Build connected research notes
use_cases.description: Create notes with aliases, tags, wikilinks, and source embeds for a research vault.

use_cases.title: Standardize project documentation
use_cases.description: Turn rough project notes into Obsidian pages with frontmatter, task lists, callouts, and related links.

use_cases.title: Prepare publishable knowledge base entries
use_cases.description: Format long notes with headings, callouts, diagrams, footnotes, and clean Obsidian-compatible structure.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple note
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian note about [topic]. Include title, tags, aliases, three sections, and links to related notes.

prompt_templates.title: Convert notes to Obsidian format
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this draft into Obsidian Flavored Markdown. Add frontmatter, wikilinks, callouts, and tags where useful: [paste draft].

prompt_templates.title: Build a project note template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian project note template with properties, status fields, task sections, embeds, and decision callouts.

prompt_templates.title: Design a connected vault entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a comprehensive Obsidian note for [subject]. Include block links, nested callouts, Mermaid diagram syntax, footnotes, and related note links.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a meeting note for a product planning session.
output_examples.output: A structured Obsidian note with frontmatter, tags, attendees, linked projects, action items, and a decision callout.

output_examples.input: A user provides a draft article and asks for Obsidian formatting.
output_examples.output: A cleaned Markdown note with wikilinks, aliases, highlights, footnotes, and section-level embeds where relevant.

output_examples.input: A user needs a research note connected to an existing vault.
output_examples.output: A research entry with source metadata, nested tags, related-note links, block references, and summary callouts.

best_practices: Use wikilinks for vault notes and standard Markdown links for external web pages.
Place YAML properties at the top of each note before the main heading.
Use callouts for warnings, decisions, summaries, and other content that should stand out.
anti_patterns: Do not use external Markdown links for notes that should be tracked inside the Obsidian vault.
Do not add complex embeds unless the linked attachment or note is expected to exist.
Do not mix many unrelated tags into one note when nested tags would be clearer.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create Obsidian Flavored Markdown notes with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, tags, comments, math, diagrams, and footnotes.

faq.question: Does it require Obsidian to run?
faq.answer: No. It provides formatting guidance, but Obsidian is needed to preview and use the rendered vault behavior.

faq.question: Can it update existing notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can rewrite existing Markdown into Obsidian-compatible structure when the note content is provided.

faq.question: Can it verify broken links?
faq.answer: Only when the relevant vault files are available to inspect. Otherwise, it can format links but cannot confirm targets.

faq.question: Does it include standard Markdown help?
faq.answer: It assumes standard Markdown knowledge and focuses on syntax specific to Obsidian.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files are Markdown documentation and examples, with no executable code or network behavior.
